---
title:        "PCA Composite Inflammatory Score"
subtitle:     "Based on Hilmer, et al., (2022). *Blood-brain barrier disruption measured by albumin index correlates with inflammatory fluid biomarkers*"
author:       Azadeh Golduzian
date:         last-modified # today, now, last-modified
date-format:  long          # full, long, medium, short, iso
format:
  html:
    theme:                  litera
    highlight-style:        atom-one
    page-layout:            full
    toc:                    true
    toc-depth:              4
    toc-location:           body
    number-sections:        true
    number-depth:           3
    code-fold:              show
    code-tools:
      toggle:               true
      caption:              "Code"
      source:               false
    code-overflow:          scroll
    code-block-bg:          true
    code-block-border-left: "#30B0E0"
    df-print:               paged
fig-width:                  8
fig-height:                 6
execute:
  cache:    false
  eval:     true
  echo:     true
---

<!---
# Compile commands in R:
  setwd("/Users/azadehgolduzian/Dropbox/2024_ADRC/Analyses/202501_ADRC_Permeability")
  fn_qmd <- "Golduzian_Rosenberg_ADRC_Inflammation_Analysis_5-PCA_20260408.qmd"
  quarto::quarto_render(input = fn_qmd)
-->

```{r init, echo=FALSE}
options(str   = strOptions(list.len = 1e3))
options(knitr.kable.NA = '')
my_seed <- 34567
set.seed(my_seed)
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Approach

PCA is run on 6 key BBB-related inflammatory biomarkers:

- **Albumin Index** (CSF BBB permeability)
- **3T MRI Permeability** (Ktrans)
- **CSF GFAP** (astrocyte activation)
- **IgG Index** (intrathecal immunoglobulin synthesis)
- **CSF IL-8** (pro-inflammatory cytokine)
- **CSF IL-6** (pro-inflammatory cytokine)

Steps:

1. Run PCA on complete cases (all 6 variables non-missing), variables centered and scaled.
2. Extract PC1 scores.
3. Compute Spearman correlation of PC1 with Albumin Index.
4. If negative, flip PC1 so higher PC1 = more BBB disruption (same direction as Albumin).
5. Correlate aligned PC1 with all 6 input variables and with diagnosis.
6. Visualize loadings, biplot, and PC1 by diagnosis group.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Data Preliminaries

```{r chunk-003}
library(erikmisc)
library(tidyverse)
library(labelled)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(patchwork)
library(factoextra)   # for fviz_pca_biplot; install if needed: install.packages("factoextra")
library(gt)
library(gtsummary)

dir.create("All_figures_for_paper", showWarnings = FALSE)
```

```{r chunk-load}
load("/Users/azadehgolduzian/Dropbox/2024_ADRC/Data/Data_out/current/ADRC4_ADRC3_MarkVCID2_VCI_ALL.RData")
load("dat_sub_Data_inflammation_analysis_paper_06052025.RData")
load("dat_sub_and_Var_for_inflammation_analysis.RData")
```

#PCA
```{r}
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PCA-A: CSF Composite Inflammatory Score for Qalb
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── Step 1: CSF predictors only ( Qalb is the target) ─────
vars_pca_csf <- c(
    # "fl_msd_csf_mmp_1_log2"
   "fl_msd_csf_mmp_2_log2"
  , "fl_msd_csf_mmp_3_log2"
  # , "fl_msd_csf_mmp_9_log2"
  # , "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2"
  
  , "fl_angio_csf_tie2_log2"
  # ,"fl_angio_csf_bfgf_log2"
  
  , "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2"
  , "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2" 
  # , "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2"
  
  # , "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2"
  # , "fl_abeta_4240_ratio_log2"
  
  # , "fl_cyto_csf_il_6_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_csf_il_8_log2"
  , "fl_cyto_csf_il_10_log2"
)

# ── Step 2: Complete cases ─────
dat_pca_csf <-
  dat_sub |>
  select(ptid, fl_albumin_index_log2, all_of(vars_pca_csf)) |>
  drop_na()

cat("N for CSF PCA:", nrow(dat_pca_csf), "\n")

# ── Step 3: Run PCA on z-scored predictors ──────────────────────────────────
pca_csf <- prcomp(
    dat_pca_csf |> select(all_of(vars_pca_csf))
  , center = TRUE
  , scale. = TRUE
)

# Variance explained
print(summary(pca_csf))
pc1_var_pct <- round(summary(pca_csf)$importance[2, 1] * 100, 1)
cat("PC1 explains", pc1_var_pct, "% of variance\n")

# Loadings 
loadings_pc1 <- pca_csf$rotation[, 1]
print(round(sort(loadings_pc1), 3))

# ── Step 4: PC1 score IS the composite inflammation score ───────────────────
dat_pca_csf$composite_csf <- pca_csf$x[, 1]

# ── Step 5: Sign convention — flip if PC1 runs opposite to Qalb ─────────────
# (PCA doesn't know which direction is "more inflammation")
cor_pc1 <- cor(
    dat_pca_csf$composite_csf
  , dat_pca_csf$fl_albumin_index_log2
  , method = "spearman"
)

if (cor_pc1 < 0) {
  dat_pca_csf$composite_csf <- -dat_pca_csf$composite_csf
  pca_csf$rotation[, 1]     <- -pca_csf$rotation[, 1]
  cat("PC1 flipped: now high composite = high BBB disruption\n")
}

# ── Step 6: Plot composite score vs Albumin Index ───────────────────────────
fit_lm   <- lm(fl_albumin_index_log2 ~ composite_csf, data = dat_pca_csf)
r2_value <- summary(fit_lm)$r.squared
cor_test <- cor.test(
    dat_pca_csf$composite_csf
  , dat_pca_csf$fl_albumin_index_log2
  , method = "spearman"
)

plot_composite_csf <-
  ggplot(dat_pca_csf, aes(x = composite_csf, y = fl_albumin_index_log2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
      title    = "CSF Composite Inflammatory Score vs Albumin Index"
    , subtitle = sprintf(
        "Spearman ρ = %.2f (p = %.3g) | R² = %.2f | n = %d | PC1 var = %.1f%%"
      , cor_test$estimate, cor_test$p.value
      , r2_value, nrow(dat_pca_csf), pc1_var_pct
      )
    , x = "CSF Composite Score (PC1)"
    , y = "Albumin Index (log2)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

print(plot_composite_csf)

# Save composite back to dat_sub for downstream use (Later)
# dat_sub <- dat_sub |>
#   left_join(dat_pca_csf |> select(ptid, composite_csf), by = "ptid")


```

```{r}
vars_pca_plasma <- c(
    # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
    # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2",
   "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_ifn_g_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_il_2_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_il_4_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_il_6_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_il_8_log2"
  , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_il_10_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_il_12p70_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_il_13_log2"
  # , "fl_cyto_heparin_plasma_tnf_a_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2"
  # , "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2"
  # , "fl_serum_igg_log2"
)


# ── Step 2: Complete cases ─────
dat_pca_csf <-
  dat_sub |>
  select(ptid, fl_albumin_index_log2, all_of(vars_pca_plasma)) |>
  drop_na()

cat("N for CSF PCA:", nrow(dat_pca_csf), "\n")

# ── Step 3: Run PCA on z-scored predictors ──────────────────────────────────
?prcomp
pca_csf <- prcomp(
    dat_pca_csf |> select(all_of(vars_pca_plasma))
  , center = TRUE
  , scale. = TRUE
)

# Variance explained
print(summary(pca_csf))
pc1_var_pct <- round(summary(pca_csf)$importance[2, 1] * 100, 1)
cat("PC1 explains", pc1_var_pct, "% of variance\n")

# Loadings 
loadings_pc1 <- pca_csf$rotation[, 1]
print(round(sort(loadings_pc1), 3))

# ── Step 4: PC1 score IS the composite inflammation score ───────────────────
dat_pca_csf$composite_csf <- pca_csf$x[, 1]

# ── Step 5: Sign convention — flip if PC1 runs opposite to Qalb ─────────────

cor_pc1 <- cor(
    dat_pca_csf$composite_csf
  , dat_pca_csf$fl_albumin_index_log2
  , method = "spearman"
)

if (cor_pc1 < 0) {
  dat_pca_csf$composite_csf <- -dat_pca_csf$composite_csf
  pca_csf$rotation[, 1]     <- -pca_csf$rotation[, 1]
  cat("PC1 flipped: now high composite = high BBB disruption\n")
}

# ── Step 6: Plot composite score vs Albumin Index ───────────────────────────
fit_lm   <- lm(fl_albumin_index_log2 ~ composite_csf, data = dat_pca_csf)
r2_value <- summary(fit_lm)$r.squared
cor_test <- cor.test(
    dat_pca_csf$composite_csf
  , dat_pca_csf$fl_albumin_index_log2
  , method = "spearman"
)

plot_composite_csf <-
  ggplot(dat_pca_csf, aes(x = composite_csf, y = fl_albumin_index_log2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
      title    = "Plasma Composite Inflammatory Score vs Albumin Index"
    , subtitle = sprintf(
        "Spearman ρ = %.2f (p = %.3g) | R² = %.2f | n = %d | PC1 var = %.1f%%"
      , cor_test$estimate, cor_test$p.value
      , r2_value, nrow(dat_pca_csf), pc1_var_pct
      )
    , x = "Plasma Composite Score (PC1)"
    , y = "Albumin Index (log2)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

print(plot_composite_csf)

# Save composite back to dat_sub for downstream use (Later)
# dat_sub <- dat_sub |>
#   left_join(dat_pca_csf |> select(ptid, composite_csf), by = "ptid")

```
